popularStory.headline: Henri Ier de Brabant (1165-1235)
popularStory.description: Henri Ier de Brabant, dit le Courageux ou le Guerroyeur, né vers 1165, mort à Cologne le 5 septembre 1235, est duc de Brabant de 1183 à 1235, comte de Louvain, marquis d'Anvers et duc de Basse-Lothari …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen於1160年3月21日出生在比利時布拉班特 瓦隆時，他的父親Godfried III van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen18歲，母親Margaretha van Limburg21歲。他於大約1179年與Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1235年9月12日在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫雷吉隆斯其可 可爾享年75歲，埋葬在比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen于1160年3月21日出生在比利时布拉班特 瓦隆时，他的父亲Godfried III van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen18岁，母亲Margaretha van Limburg21岁。他于大约1179年与Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1235年9月12日在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫雷吉隆斯其可 可爾享年75岁，埋葬在比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Brabant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Aristide, Marcel, Normand, Ovila, Yvan.
French, Walloon, Flemish, and German: habitational name from Brabant, a province in the Low Countries.

English: cognate of 1 above, from Old French Brabant, Middle English Braban, Brabayn, used to denote both the region and an inhabitant of it. See also the synonymous Brabazon . In medieval England, Brabans were typically linen weavers and cloth merchants. The final syllable of Braban, when weakly stressed, was often altered to -ben, -bin, or -bon, and also to -bam. The additional -t or -d in post-medieval Brabant, Braband , etc. is probably excrescent, rather than a relic of the Old French form.

variants.name: Brabender
meanings: German (mainly Westphalia): habitational name for someone from Brabant (see   Brabant  ), perhaps applied also as a nickname for a merchant of Brabantine cloth.

variants.name: Brabazon
meanings: English: from Old French Brabançon, Anglo-Norman French Brabanzon, Middle English Brab(a)son ‘Brabanter’. This was originally an ethnic name for a native of the duchy of Brabant (now in north Belgium). Medieval Brabant was widely known for two exports, its brutal mercenary soldiers and its linen cloth. In the 13th century parts of France were devastated by marauding gangs of armed Brabanters, and brabançon came to be used generically to denote a mercenary, hired assassin, or robber. These originated in Brabant and Flanders, but in the course of time accepted recruits from anywhere. The true source of the surname is the presence in many English (and Irish) towns of Brabant cloth merchants.

variants.name: Bremner
meanings: Scottish: habitational name for someone from Brabant in the Low Countries, from Older Scots Brebner, Brabanare ‘native or inhabitant of Brabant’ (see Brabant and compare with the synonymous Brabazon ). Weavers from Brabant settled early at Aberdeen and on the east coast of Scotland.
German: habitational name for someone from the city of Bremen. This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Brabham
meanings: English: variant of Brabant , altered by association with English habitational names ending in -ham.

variants.name: Braband
meanings: German and English: variant of   Brabant  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Hendrik
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

en.lifeSummary: When Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen was born on 21 March 1160, in Walloon Brabant, Belgium, his father, Godfried III van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, was 18 and his mother, Margaretha van Limburg, was 21. He married Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace about 1179. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 12 September 1235, in Cologne Region, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, at the age of 75, and was buried in Leuven, Flemish Brabant, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen est né le 21 mars 1160, à Brabant wallon, Belgique, son père, Godfried III van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Margaretha van Limburg, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace environ 1179. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 12 septembre 1235, à Regierungsbezirk Köln, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Leuven, Brabant Flamand, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen am 21. März 1160 in Wallonisch Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Godfried III van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen 18 und seine Mutter Margaretha van Limburg 21. Er hat um 1179 Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 12. September 1235 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Regierungsbezirk Köln, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland und wurde in Leuven, Flämisch Brabant, Belgien begraben.

it.Bergen op Zoom, Noord-Brabant, Netherlan: Bergen op Zoom, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi
it.lifeSummary: Quando Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen è nato il il 21 marzo 1160 nel Brabant wallon, Belgio, il suo padre, Godfried III van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, aveva 18 anni e il suo madre, Margaretha van Limburg, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace circa 1179. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 12 settembre 1235, nel Regierungsbezirk Köln, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolto a Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingenが1160年3月21日にベルギーブラバント ワロンで生まれた時，父Godfried III van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingenは18歳，母Margaretha van Limburgは21歳であった。約1179年にMathilde de Boulogne-Alsaceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1235年9月12日にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンレジエルングスベズイルク クoルンで75歳で死去し，ベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントレウベンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen(이)가 1160년 3월 21일 벨기에 브라반트 월런에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Godfried III van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaretha van Limburg(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1179년 Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1235년 9월 12일 독일 노르드하이-베스트파렌 레기런그스베지르크 ko에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 블라아스-브라반트 레우븐에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen nasceu em 21 de março de 1160, em Brabant wallon, Bélgica, seu pai, Godfried III van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Margaretha van Limburg, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace aproximadamente 1179. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 12 de setembro de 1235, em Regierungsbezirk Köln, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha, com 75 anos, e foi sepultado em Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica.

ro.Bergen op Zoom, Noord-Brabant, Netherlan: Bergen op Zoom, Noord-Brabant, Ţările de Jos

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen родился 21 марта 1160 г. в местности брабант валлон, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Godfried III van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaretha van Limburg, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace около 1179 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 12 сентября 1235 г. в местности региирунгсбезирк кoлн, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности леувен, влаамс-брабант, Бельгия.

es.21 MAR 1160–12 SEP 1235: 21 de marzo de 1160–12 de septiembre de 1235
es.Bergen op Zoom, Noord-Brabant, Netherlan: Bergen op Zoom, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hendrik I van Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen nació el 21 de marzo de 1160, en Brabant wallon, Bélgica, su papá, Godfried III van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Margaretha van Limburg, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Mathilde de Boulogne-Alsace aproximadamente 1179. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 12 de septiembre de 1235, en Regierungsbezirk Köln, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica.
